reviews.review_msg: I failed and I'm going to retake the course, take my advice: (1) start the homework super early. It's easy marks and you will need them. (2) Same with the quizzes (3) flashcards, study daily (4) the second you don't understand something, look it up. Notation is hard at first.
The final average was around 40%, so yeah… it was pretty brutal. Way harder than the midterm, not even comparable. Definitely be ready for a tough final.

She recently came to SFU to teach a few classes. This particular class was adapted from Dan Boneh's highly-rated online cryptography class you can find on Coursera, using his slide set with modifications. Quizzes are online, and exams are based on quizzes, and only done after the quiz solutions are discussed in class. Overall her delivery is good.
